posts.name: Steve L Gellman
posts.cooked: Just curious what is the oldest bottle you own? My would be a 86 Phelps Backus. Probably should have been drunk by now!

posts.name: Joe B
posts.cooked: I have a 1995 boujoilais from France and a 1994 Dowes port.

posts.name: Robert Love
posts.cooked: 1970 Giuseppe Rinaldi Barolo.

posts.name: D Borczon
posts.cooked: No, those old Phelps age well! That vineyard and the winemaking style in the 80s was rather conservative. Might be just delightful…

posts.name: David Bueker
posts.cooked: 1966 Ports

posts.name: Bruce Klen
posts.cooked: Don’t have a wine cellar . Mine is 2010. If did have a celllar don’t think I’d have anything older anyway.

posts.name: Mark Golodetz
posts.cooked:
Fortified: 1830 Quinta de Serrado malmsey
Table wine: 1911 Domaine Jean Morin NSG Les Cailles

Does an 1827 Solera count?

posts.name: Dale Williams
posts.cooked:
My wife is a 1959, have several '59 Bdx, plus a Moulin Touchais and one Piedmont.
I’m a '60, so have some ports (and 1 BV GdlT left)
Oldest I’ve owned were a couple bottles of '29 Poujeaux, but they’re history

posts.name: Tim McCracken
posts.cooked:
This:

Red wine, the rest unknown.

Oldest known bottle: 1889 Gruaud Larose
Oldest I’ve owned besides the one pictured: 1846 Madeira Bual

posts.name: Paul Mc_C_ourt
posts.cooked: I have some Bordeaux from the 80’s. some 90’s CDP and Huet. Rest is pretty young.

posts.name: Joe Raymond
posts.cooked: 1989 Lynch Bages

posts.name: Scott Butler
posts.cooked:
1994 Ridge Geyserville.

In the past 2 years I’ve consumed from my cellar:
1985 Ridge Geyserville
1991 Inglenook Cabernet
1994 Kopke Porto Colheita
1994 Mount Eden Vineyards Chardonnay Santa Cruz Mountains
1995 Casanova di Neri Brunello di Montalcino Tenuta Nuova


I am currently pretty low on older bottles.

posts.name: Rich Brown
posts.cooked:
1900 Justinos Madeira

And for table wine, probably the old California Burgundys I acquired from the UC Davis cellar earlier this year. Circa 1930’s

posts.name: William Kelley
posts.cooked: An 1815 Bual Madeira and a smattering of postwar Burgundies and Bordeaux back to 1945.

posts.name: Rich Brown
posts.cooked:
Tim McCracken:

This:
Red wine, the rest unknown.

Oldest known bottle: 1889 Gruaud Larose
Oldest I’ve owned besides the one pictured: 1846 Madeira Bual

That’s awesome! Where’d you get that thing?? Any idea at all where it’s from?

posts.name: suzanne_camhi
posts.cooked:
1946 CVNE Viña Real Gran Reserva- which is getting opened tonight
1949 Jean Bourdy Chateau-Chalon

posts.name: Ben Sherwin
posts.cooked:
1969 G. Conterno Barolo

I have a few from the '70s, with the prize being a mag of 1975 Mayacamas Cab.
1983 Chateau Lascombes

posts.name: Robert Alfert
posts.cooked: 1931 and 1934 Collares

posts.name: Matt Citrang10
posts.cooked:
Forgot about this one,

Cantina Abbazia (in San Stefano Belbo) 1954 Barolo

title: What's The Oldest Bottle In Your Cellar?
